subject: Cloud Computing
title: Container Orchestration
description: A tiny lesson with a big payoff: Container Orchestration.

hook.text: Running one container is easy. Running 10,000 containers across 500 servers — that needs orchestration.

buildup.text: Container orchestrators like Kubernetes schedule containers, restart crashed ones, and scale with demand.

discovery.text: Managed services — EKS, AKS, GKE — run Kubernetes for you. The control plane is the provider's problem.

twist.text: Kubernetes is powerful but complex. Many teams underestimate the learning curve and operational overhead.

climax.text: If you need it, Kubernetes is unmatched. If you don't, simpler tools like ECS or Cloud Run save you the headache.

punchline.text: Kubernetes orchestrates at scale. But simpler options exist.

quiz.question: What is the main role of a container orchestrator?
options: Scheduling, scaling, and managing the lifecycle of containers across servers
Converting virtual machines into containers automatically
Encrypting all data stored inside containers
